name: Thin Mint Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush Thin Mint cookies and spread evenly in bottom of a 9-inch Springform pan.
2. For the first layer filling, beat cream cheese until smooth, then add sugar and mix well. Stir in melted chocolate chips, crushed Thin Mints, eggs, and vanilla. Pour into prepared crust and place in freezer to firm up.
3. For the second layer filling, beat cream cheese until smooth. Add sugar, remaining chocolate chips, sour cream, eggs, and peppermint extract. Mix until well combined.
4. Carefully spread the second layer over the cooled first layer. Place the pan back in the freezer.
5. Bake at 325°F for 1-1/2 hours, or until the top feels jiggly but firm.
6. Remove from oven and let cool. Refrigerate before serving.
7. For the topping, melt chocolate chips and whisk in cream with peppermint extract. Spread over the cooled cake while still runny. Decorate with cookies and add green icing around them. Keep chilled.
Source: Abilene Reporter-News, Silvetta, Cypress Street Station
